name: squad-build
description: Reference implementation and documentation for building AMESA orchestrations and simulators using the AMESA SDK.

publishing-docs.description: Granular publishing/reference docs split by component and concern to improve targeted retrieval by analysis agents.

files.overview.md: What AgentTeacher is for and when to use it
files.reference.md: Full AgentTeacher scaffold with all required/optional methods, functional intent, capability notes, and :raises: entries covering NotImplementedError, AttributeError on non-array filtered sensors, and ValueError on action-mask length mismatches
files.job-json-schema.md: Serialized teacher agent config shape and field intent
files.publishing.md: pyproject.toml portable component spec for agent-teacher
files.quirks.md: Behavior caveats and common failure modes

files.reference.md: Full AgentController scaffold with all required/optional methods, intended use, capabilities, and :raises: entries covering NotImplementedError on each abstract method
files.scenarios.md: Scenario usage pattern for controller-backed agents
files.publishing.md: pyproject.toml portable component spec for agent-controller

coordinated.description: AgentCoach / coordinated set/population docs split by concern.

files.overview.md: Core coordinated types and use cases
files.reference.md: Full AgentCoach scaffold with all required/optional methods and team-level functional behavior
files.child-agents.md: Child agent registration and naming constraints
files.publishing.md: pyproject.toml portable component spec for coordinated agents

files.reference.md: Full PerceptorImpl scaffold with complete method contract, feature-derivation intent, and :raises: entries covering NotImplementedError, key-collision Exception, and ValueError on None leaf values
files.publishing.md: pyproject.toml portable component spec for perceptors

goals.description: Goal system docs split by concern.

files.overview.md: Goal system purpose and supported goal classes
files.reference.md: Full goal-backed teacher scaffold with required/optional overrides and method intent

files.overview.md: Orchestrator purpose, routing model, and SDK compliance essentials
files.reference.md: Teacher/controller orchestrator scaffolds with orchestrator-specific method contracts and :raises: entries for abstract methods, action-mask length mismatches, and AttributeError on non-array filtered sensors
files.job-json-schema.md: Serialized orchestrator config shapes for AgentOrchestrator and AgentOrchestratorController
files.publishing.md: Scaffold, package metadata, compliance checklist, and orchestrator publish command
files.quirks.md: Orchestrator-specific caveats around type names, child indexing, and action masks

examples.description: Uploadable example components, one per publishable type. Each subdirectory is a self-contained package ready for amesa_cli agent publish.

teacher-example.description: AgentTeacher example: ReactorTemperatureTeacher regulates reactor temperature toward a setpoint.

files.teacher_example/teacher.py: ReactorTemperatureTeacher — reward shaped on absolute temperature error, success within 1 °C of setpoint, termination on unsafe temperature

controller-example.description: AgentController example: PDController implements a proportional-derivative law for a 1-D position servo.

files.controller_example/controller.py: PDController — force = -(Kp * position + Kd * velocity), success at |position| < 0.05 m, termination at |position| > 10 m

coordinated-example.description: AgentCoach (coordinated set) example: DriveCoach coordinates a navigate sub-agent and an avoid sub-agent for autonomous driving.

files.coordinated_example/coach.py: DriveCoach — distributes independent rewards to 'navigate' (distance-to-goal) and 'avoid' (obstacle clearance) sub-agents

perceptors-example.description: PerceptorImpl example: KinematicsPerceptor derives velocity and acceleration from consecutive position readings.

files.pyproject.toml: Package metadata; [amesa] type = perceptor, variables = [velocity, acceleration]
files.perceptors_example/perceptor.py: KinematicsPerceptor — finite-difference velocity and acceleration from position + dt, resets on step_count == 0

goals-example.description: Goal example: LandingGoal is a CoordinatedGoal (AND) combining ApproachGoal (reach altitude 0) and AvoidGoal (limit descent speed) for a soft-landing task.

files.pyproject.toml: Package metadata; [amesa] type = agent-teacher (Goal subclasses are AgentTeacher)
files.goals_example/goal.py: LandingGoal — ApproachGoal on altitude (target 0, boundary ±0.5 m, scale 2) + AvoidGoal on descent_velocity (boundary 0–5 m/s), AND strategy, weights [2, 1]

orchestrator-example.description: Orchestrator example: CounterOrchestratorTeacher chooses a child agent index from demand and error signals.

files.orchestrator_example/teacher.py: CounterOrchestratorTeacher — routes between child indices with optional masking; reward favors high demand and low error

analysis.description: Analyst-focused reference docs and sample generated artifacts for inspecting benchmark and historian outputs.

files.benchmark-json.md: How to analyze benchmark.json: structure, fields, aggregates, replay usage, and interpretation patterns.
files.historian-delta.md: How to analyze historian Delta Lake output: schema, event catalog, query patterns, and artifact differences vs benchmark output.

benchmarking-sample.description: Sample benchmark export for a CartPole-like run.

files.benchmark.json: Scenario/episode/step benchmark output with aggregate stats.

historian-sample.description: Sample historian Delta Lake output for run-cartpole-abc123.

run-cartpole-abc123.description: Delta Lake table directory for one sample run.

files.part-00000-dd918d2e-f3f8-41f7-b1c1-144d3: Sample telemetry data batch in Parquet format.

files.preview.json: Small JSON preview of the first historian rows for quick inspection.

root_files.manifest.json: This file — repo structure and file descriptions
